name: Travis AFB
profile: Military-friendly Travis AFB housing snapshot anchored to Fairfield, Vacaville, Dixon, and Solano County, built for PCSUnited base-level market overviews.

market_metrics.market_type_summary: High-demand California military housing market with high entry costs and strong regional price pressure

mortgage_assumptions.defaults_note: Based on March 2026 California VA loan planning assumptions.

notes: Travis is a high-cost California military housing market where purchase price, taxes, insurance, utilities, commute, and BAH must be evaluated together.
Insurance and HOA assumptions should be included in all-in monthly housing cost comparisons.
High home values can make ownership realistic only for disciplined buyers with clear assignment-length and exit-strategy planning.

avg_home_mortgage_monthly.notes: VA loan planning estimate; high entry costs require significant BAH alignment and strong residual-income discipline.

crime_status.installation: Extremely safe; gated military community with controlled access.
crime_status.metro_fairfield_vacaville: Fairfield has stronger and weaker corridors that require neighborhood-level screening; Vacaville and Dixon are often compared for family fit, commute, and perceived safety.

school_quality.on_base: Travis Unified School District serves many on-base and Travis-connected families; school assignment should still be verified by exact address.
school_quality.off_base: Vacaville Unified, Fairfield-Suisun Unified, Travis Unified, Dixon Unified, and nearby districts vary by address; families should verify school zones and program fit before choosing housing.

rental_vacancy.notes: Tight rental market; demand is influenced by Travis AFB, Solano County employment, and broader Bay Area housing pressure.

rental_metrics.notes: RentCast July 2026 market data shows elevated rents across selected Travis PCS housing ZIPs, with median rent near $2,570.

military_lifestyle_fit.description: Travis has a strong military presence and sits between the Bay Area and Sacramento, offering access to major employment, services, and lifestyle options while requiring disciplined housing-cost planning.

financial_brief.affordability_summary: High cost of living requires careful budgeting even with California BAH rates. Travis users should compare rent, mortgage, taxes, insurance, utilities, commute, debt, and assignment length before deciding.
financial_brief.buyer_opportunity: Strongest for disciplined buyers comparing Fairfield, Vacaville, Dixon, and nearby corridors by commute, school zones, and all-in monthly payment.

market_bluf.bluf_summary: Travis offers stable regional housing demand but high entry costs relative to many Air Force markets. The best decisions depend on BAH fit, commute, school zones, and assignment length.

summary_points: Travis AFB sits in a high-cost Solano County housing market anchored by Fairfield, Vacaville, Dixon, Suisun City, and nearby Bay Area corridors.
Current market trend is approximately 0.8% year-over-year, which suggests relative stability but limited affordability relief.
High cost of living requires careful budgeting even with California BAH rates.
Neighborhood quality, school zones, commute, and carrying costs vary materially by exact address.
Travis can work for buyers seeking long-term equity, but short assignments or tight monthly budgets may favor renting.
opportunities: Travis military demand can support durable housing relevance for disciplined buyers and landlords.
Fairfield, Vacaville, Dixon, and Suisun City give users multiple commute and price-point options.
Longer assignment timelines may support ownership for households that can handle California carrying costs.
Military-friendly rental demand may benefit clean, well-located homes near practical commute corridors.
Regional access to the Bay Area and Sacramento can support long-term market stability.
risks: High purchase prices can compress monthly affordability even when BAH appears strong.
Property taxes, insurance, HOA, utilities, and maintenance can widen the gap between list price and true monthly payment.
Neighborhood quality, schools, commute, and safety vary enough to require address-level review.
Short assignments can increase resale and transaction-cost risk.
Some BAH profiles may be better served by renting than buying in this market.
buyer_notes: Travis buyers should compare all-in monthly cost, not just principal and interest.
Verify school zones, commute timing, property taxes, insurance, HOA, and BAH fit by exact address before signing.
Vacaville and Dixon may offer stronger family or value positioning for some households, while Fairfield may offer commute convenience.
This market rewards disciplined buyers who test the commute during expected report hours.
Do not buy simply because the property is close to Travis; test monthly payment, exit strategy, and lifestyle fit.
seller_notes: Sellers should price against current inventory and days on market rather than older peak assumptions.
Move-in-ready presentation, realistic pricing, and clear commute positioning matter when buyers have more options.
Military-adjacent convenience can be a listing strength if framed accurately.
Homes with clear school-zone, commute, and maintenance advantages may stand out to PCS buyers.
investor_angles: Military turnover near Travis can support rental demand, but vacancy, repairs, taxes, insurance, and turnover should be modeled conservatively.
Practical commute corridors often outperform generic metro averages for military renter demand.
High acquisition costs require disciplined underwriting and realistic rent assumptions.
Investors should focus on clean condition, functional layouts, and durable rental appeal rather than appreciation assumptions alone.
buyer_guidance: Use all-in monthly payment as the primary screen.
Compare taxes, insurance, commute, school quality, safety, and resale flexibility before locking a neighborhood.
Do not choose housing based only on distance to Travis.
A slightly lower purchase price can materially improve monthly PCS flexibility.
Use PCSUnited calculators to compare rent, buy, residual income, assignment length, and exit risk before committing.
seller_guidance: Price against active inventory and days on market.
Condition and presentation matter when buyers can compare more options.
Military commute convenience can help positioning if described accurately.
PCS buyers need practical monthly value, clear property condition, and confidence in the exit plan.
landlord_notes: Military renter demand can improve occupancy durability near Travis.
Model vacancy, repairs, insurance, HOA, property management, and turnover conservatively.
Stable spread matters more than headline affordability alone.
Homes with practical commute access, clean condition, and functional layouts are better positioned for military tenants.

market.market_type_summary: High-demand California military housing market with high entry costs and strong regional price pressure

base_profile.host_or_major_command: 60th Air Mobility Wing / Air Mobility Command
base_profile.primary_mission_summary: Travis AFB supports air mobility, airlift, refueling, medical evacuation, logistics, and mission support in Solano County between the Bay Area and Sacramento.
base_profile.pcs_personality: High-cost, mission-heavy, family-relevant, commute-sensitive, school-zone-sensitive, and strongly influenced by Fairfield, Vacaville, Dixon, Suisun City, and broader Bay Area housing pressure.
base_profile.base_bluf: Travis is not just a California BAH decision. It is a high-cost housing, commute, school-zone, assignment-length, and exit-strategy decision. The market can support long-term equity, but only when the user controls monthly payment and chooses the right corridor.

user_positioning.military_family_bluf: Prioritize school-zone verification, BAH fit, childcare access, commute testing, medical access, and total monthly housing cost before signing a lease or purchase contract near Travis.
user_positioning.single_airman_bluf: Prioritize commute simplicity, rental flexibility, affordability, roommate options, and access to daily services around Fairfield and Vacaville.
user_positioning.realtor_bluf: The strongest Travis housing conversations are about BAH realism, monthly payment discipline, commute risk, school-zone confidence, and all-in cost math.
user_positioning.investor_bluf: Rental demand near Travis is influenced by PCS turnover, California housing pressure, limited affordability, and practical commute corridors.

Users who want predictable commute and base services access

School assignment must be verified by exact address
Off-base housing costs can be high enough that on-base housing should be part of the comparison
California utility, insurance, and commute costs should be considered before choosing off-base distance
on_base_housing.pcsu_strategy_note: Compare on-base housing against off-base rent and mortgage options near Travis. Around Travis, the best choice depends on rank, dependents, BAH, debt, commute tolerance, school priorities, family support needs, pet needs, spouse employment, and expected assignment length.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Fairfield
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Fairfield is the closest major city corridor for Travis and can offer commute convenience, but users must screen by exact neighborhood, school zone, safety, and property condition.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 10-25 minutes depending on exact address, gate, traffic, and report time
likely_gate_logic: Commuter Gate depending on route and current access rules
watchouts: Neighborhood quality can vary materially
School assignment should be verified by exact address
Property condition and monthly carrying costs vary by street and subdivision
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Strong first-look area for users wanting practical Travis access, but exact-address due diligence is required.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Vacaville
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Vacaville is a common Travis PCS research corridor with strong family appeal, services, and commute tradeoffs.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 15-35 minutes depending on exact address, gate, and traffic
likely_gate_logic: Commuter Gate depending on route and current access rules
watchouts: Higher prices can pressure payment discipline
School zones must be verified by exact address
Commute should be tested during expected report hours
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Strong candidate for families and long-term buyers who can support the payment and value the lifestyle fit.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Dixon
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Dixon can provide a smaller-community alternative for Travis households willing to trade distance for value or lifestyle fit.
best_for: Buyers comparing value outside Fairfield and Vacaville
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 25-45 minutes depending on exact address, gate, and traffic
likely_gate_logic: Commuter Gate depending on route and current access rules
watchouts: Longer commute than many Fairfield options
Inventory and property type may be limited
Route and gate access should be verified
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Useful alternative for users who prefer Dixon lifestyle or pricing and accept the commute tradeoff.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Suisun City
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Suisun City can offer relative value and practical access to Travis, but users should compare neighborhood quality, schools, and commute carefully.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 15-35 minutes depending on exact address, gate, and traffic
likely_gate_logic: Commuter Gate depending on route and current access rules
watchouts: Neighborhood-level screening matters
School zones should be verified by exact address
Property condition and resale should be reviewed carefully
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Can work for value-focused users when the exact property, commute, and neighborhood fit are strong.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Benicia Corridor
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: The Benicia corridor may offer lifestyle and regional access advantages, but it can increase commute and cost tradeoffs for Travis households.
best_for: Users wanting broader Bay Area access
Families comparing school and community fit
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 35-55+ minutes depending on exact address, gate, and traffic
likely_gate_logic: Commuter Gate depending on route and current access rules
watchouts: Longer commute can become painful with early report times
Higher costs may weaken BAH fit
Lifestyle benefits must justify commute and payment tradeoffs
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Only recommended for users who intentionally want the corridor and have tested the commute and monthly payment.

commute_intelligence.overall_commute_posture: Mixed compared with many military markets because close-in options exist, but traffic, gate access, Bay Area influence, school drop-off timing, and exact address can materially change the daily commute.
primary_commute_risks: Choosing by mileage instead of report-time commute
Traffic from Fairfield, Vacaville, Dixon, Suisun City, and Bay Area corridors
Higher-cost neighborhoods that still create commute friction
planning_guidance: Test the commute during the user’s expected report-time window.
Confirm which gate best supports the user’s duty location.
Compare Fairfield, Vacaville, Dixon, Suisun City, and nearby corridors by route, services, schools, safety, and resale.
Use commute plus all-in housing cost as the core PCSUnited decision screen.
commute_intelligence.pcsu_bluf: For Travis, gate access and traffic timing often matter more than straight-line distance. The best housing choice balances BAH fit, commute, schools, and assignment length.

bah_market_reality.overall_position: Neutral for buyers and renters, with high-cost-market caution.
bah_market_reality.buying_power_summary: California BAH can help, but high home values around Fairfield, Vacaville, Dixon, and Solano County can still create payment pressure for many households.
bah_market_reality.renting_summary: Renting remains practical for shorter assignments, users with tight residual income, or households that do not want California resale exposure.
bah_market_reality.pcsu_strategy_note: Travis should be modeled as a high-cost but stable market. PCSUnited should test each user’s rank, BAH, debt, savings, credit score, timeline, family needs, and exit plan before recommending buy versus rent.

strengths: Access to Fairfield, Vacaville, Dixon, and broader Solano County services
Regional access to Sacramento and the Bay Area
On-base housing can simplify the PCS for some families
watchouts: High housing costs can pressure monthly budget
School assignments and program fit should be verified by exact address
Childcare availability and waitlists should be checked early
Commute and traffic can matter more than expected
Insurance, taxes, HOA, and utilities can materially change the true payment
family_readiness.pcsu_family_bluf: Travis can work well for families who value services, schools, and regional access, but it requires disciplined housing-cost planning and exact-address verification.

arrival_checklist: Verify current gate hours and visitor access procedures through official Travis AFB sources.
Confirm whether on-base housing or off-base housing better fits rank, dependents, BAH, pets, school needs, spouse employment, and expected assignment length.
Compare Fairfield, Vacaville, Dixon, Suisun City, and other corridors using commute, schools, safety, services, and all-in monthly cost.
Review taxes, insurance, HOA, utilities, property condition, and commute route before buying.
Verify school zones by exact property address.
Test the commute during the expected report-time window before signing.
Use PCSUnited calculators to compare rent, buy, residual income, assignment length, and exit risk before committing.
pcs_watchouts: Do not assume California BAH automatically makes buying affordable.
Do not choose only by distance to Travis.
Do not ignore taxes, insurance, HOA, utilities, and maintenance.
Verify schools, commute, and property condition by exact address before committing.
Short assignments may favor renting or on-base housing if resale risk is high.
High-cost markets require stronger residual-income discipline.

realtor_intelligence.best_client_positioning: Lead with BAH realism, commute testing, monthly payment discipline, school-zone verification, and exit strategy. Travis clients need grounded housing math, not generic California market advice.
buyer_consultation_angles: Rent versus buy based on assignment length
All-in payment including taxes, insurance, HOA, utilities, and maintenance
Fairfield versus Vacaville versus Dixon lifestyle tradeoffs
Property condition and VA appraisal readiness
Future resale appeal to military buyers
realtor_intelligence.pcsu_realtor_bluf: A strong Travis realtor should sound like a BAH strategist, commute filter, school-zone guide, and high-cost-market risk manager — not just a sales agent.

notes: Market and housing values reflect July 2026 RentCast market data across selected Travis PCS housing ZIPs.
Gate, visitor, service, medical, and housing office details should be verified against official installation sources before publication or use in routing.
Placeholder map image is used until a dedicated Travis base orientation map is created.
Travis market guidance should account for Fairfield, Vacaville, Dixon, Suisun City, and Bay Area-influenced housing pressure.

source_notes: Use official Travis AFB sources for gate hours, visitor control, newcomer guidance, and installation access.
Use official Travis housing, Air Force Housing, and privatized housing sources for on-base housing and housing-office details.
Use DeCA, AAFES, and TRICARE sources for commissary, exchange, and medical details.
Use RentCast market data, Census demographic data, MLS-style housing data, and local rental listing observations for housing and market refreshes.
base_profile.disclaimer: PCSUnited base profiles are planning tools only. They are not official DoD, Air Force, installation, legal, financial, real estate, lending, or navigation guidance. Users should verify gate access, housing rules, school zones, medical services, visitor procedures, market prices, insurance estimates, commute conditions, and property condition through official and professional sources before making decisions.

base_profile.provider: PCSUnited planning profile using public-facing installation, housing, and market research structure

parser_notes: Built in the PCSUnited city/base JSON pattern used by Cannon, Lackland, Andrews, Barksdale, and Beale.
All major PCSUnited fields included and adapted for Travis AFB.
Compatible with PCS Snapshot, Base Demographics Shell, Base SaaS, and TOR parsing.
Preserves duplicate value aliases for avg_home_value, average_home_value, avgHome, and city_avg_home.
Preserves duplicate median price aliases for median_list_price, median_sold_price, and median_sale_price.
Preserves by_bedroom entries for 2, 3, 4, and 5-bedroom Market Insight compatibility.

Expanded base_profile to include map image, official links, visitor control center, gates, major services, on-base housing, recommended neighborhoods, commute intelligence, BAH market reality, family readiness, arrival checklist, PCS watchouts, realtor intelligence, data confidence, source notes, and disclaimer.

RentCast July 2026 values are retained for median sale price, median list price, rental metrics, active listings, days on market, and selected housing-market ZIP summaries through sources.housing_market raw file references.
